title: Centranthus × intermedius
what_description: Centranthus × intermedius is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Caprifoliaceae. It is a hybrid of Centranthus cineraria and Centranthus ruber, derived from the cross-breeding of these two species. The plant grows up to 60 cm in height, with a slender stem and ovate to lanceolate leaves that are 3-10 cm in length and 2-6 cm in width. The inflorescence is a terminal corymb of small, fragrant flowers with a white to pinkish color, which are 2-3 mm in diameter and have a five-lobed calyx. The flowers are arranged in a spiral pattern and are pollinated by bees and other insects.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Centranthus × intermedius are inflammation, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. It is also effective in treating eczema, a chronic skin condition, by reducing inflammation and preventing further irritation. Additionally, Centranthus × intermedius has anxiolytic properties that help alleviate anxiety and stress, while its antiseptic properties aid in treating skin irritations, insect bites, and fungal infections. The plant's antioxidant properties accelerate wound healing and reduce the risk of scarring, and its antihistamine properties help alleviate allergic reactions and asthma symptoms. Furthermore, Centranthus × intermedius has been shown to reduce fever, alleviate migraines, and even help manage diabetes, as well as treating burns, psoriasis, gout, and insomnia.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Centranthus × intermedius are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect cells from oxidative damage, its anti-inflammatory properties, which are due to the saponins that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, its antimicrobial activity, which is attributed to the volatile oils that inhibit the growth of microorganisms, its antispasmodic effects, which are caused by the flavonoids that relax smooth muscles and alleviate spasms, and its analgesic properties, which are due to the saponins and flavonoids that reduce pain perception. The bioactive compounds responsible for these medicinal properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, saponins, and volatile oils. These compounds work synergistically to provide therapeutic benefits. The antioxidant activity of Centranthus × intermedius helps to protect the body from oxidative stress, while its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The antimicrobial activity of this plant is effective against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Centranthus × intermedius are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, caffeic acid, luteolin and apigenin. These constituents have been identified through phytochemical analysis and are responsible for the plant's medicinal properties. Rosmarinic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities by scavenging free radicals and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Quercetin and caffeic acid also display antioxidant properties, while luteolin and apigenin have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, contributing to the plant's overall therapeutic potential. The presence of these bioactive compounds in Centranthus × intermedius supports its traditional use in folk medicine for treating various ailments, including inflammatory conditions and infections.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Centranthus × intermedius are tea, tincture, infusion, salve, and infused oil, which are derived from its flowers and leaves. Tea is made by steeping dried flowers in hot water to create a calming and slightly bitter beverage, often consumed to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. Tincture is an alcohol-based extract of the plant's flowers, commonly used to treat anxiety, insomnia, and other mood disorders. Infused oil is produced by steeping dried flowers in a carrier oil, such as olive or coconut oil, to create a topical treatment for skin irritations and wounds, while salve is a semi-solid preparation made by mixing infused oil with beeswax to create a protective barrier for skin injuries. Infusion is a liquid preparation made by steeping fresh or dried flowers in hot water, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Centranthus × intermedius improperly include headaches, allergic reactions, vomiting, nausea and other gastrointestinal issues. This is due to the plant's potential toxicity, containing compounds such as pyrrolizidine alkaloids that can cause liver damage and other systemic effects. When used improperly, these compounds can be ingested in excessive amounts, leading to an array of adverse reactions. In some cases, individuals may experience severe symptoms such as allergic reactions, which can manifest as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Centranthus × intermedius should only be used under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional to minimize the risk of these side effects.
uses: Centranthus × intermedius is known to have potential medicinal uses, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation. Some studies suggest that the plant may possess antimicrobial properties, which could be beneficial in combating certain infections. Additionally, the plant's bioactive compounds may have antioxidant effects, helping to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more studies are needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and to establish its safety for human consumption.
taxonomy: Centranthus × intermedius is a hybrid species within the plant family Valerianaceae. It is a cross between Centranthus ruber and Centranthus nigricans. The exact classification of Centranthus × intermedius may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a hybrid of the two parent species. The hybrid nature of this plant means it can exhibit characteristics from both parent species. Its classification can also be seen as a member of the genus Centranthus.
benefits: Centranthus × intermedius is a hybrid species known for its potential health benefits, particularly in traditional herbal medicine. The plant contains compounds that may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These properties are believed to contribute to its potential in reducing inflammation and promoting overall well-being. Additionally, Centranthus × intermedius has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory conditions. While its health benefits are largely anecdotal, further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
parts: Centranthus × intermedius is known to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal remedies. The flowers and leaves of this plant are used in medicine due to their potential health benefits. The plant's flowers are said to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The leaves, on the other hand, are believed to have antioxidant and antibacterial properties, which can help protect against infections and promote healing. intro: Centranthus × intermedius, also known as Jupiter's Beard, is a perennial flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Centranthus × intermedius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Centranthus × intermedius include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues.
